methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Extropic is a Series A chip and semiconductor company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductors players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip firms, with particular focus on same-stage and adjacent-stage peers including Substrate ($1.0B, Series A), Etched ($5.0B, Series B), and Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $50M total raised proxy, implying a $100M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $1.0B implied valuation, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The sector median P/S of 23.4x across public semiconductors provides context but lacks the necessary input.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $85M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $550M. This wide range reflects tension between a conservative stage-based approach and peer multiples skewed upward by later-stage outliers like Etched. Key uncertainties include the maturity and commercialization timeline of Extropic's core technology, the relevance of Series B comparables to current Series A valuation, and whether raised capital adequately proxies post-money value.
